'''Baraduk''' was a [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] from [[Barab I/Legends|Barab I]]. Captured by [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]] and sent to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] settlement of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Herios IV]], Baraduk raided the settler's armory and escaped. During several months, the [[Unidentified Imperial governor (Herios IV)|Imperial governor]] vainly tried to capture Baraduk, and eventually pardoned the Barabel should he leave the planet.

Baraduk began a life of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]], sometimes resorting to working as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]]. He specialized in night attacks, making use of his [[Species/Legends|species]]' natural infrared vision. He sent his income to Barab I, to help other Barabels wiping Imperial presence from his homeworld. The [[Kerestian/Legends|Kerestian]] bounty hunter [[Galasett]] tracked and fought Baraduk, but he was unable to capture him in their first match.

==Biography==
Baraduk was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] born in the Barabel [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], [[Barab I/Legends|Barab I]]. During his time, some elements of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] exploited the Barabel [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]], sometimes resorting to [[Slavery/Legends|enslaving]] Barabels. Baraduk was captured by slavers and sent to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Herios IV]], seat of an Imperial settlement.<ref name="G66" />

Once there, Baraduk decided to escape. He stole several weapons from the settlement's armory,<ref name="G66" /> including a portable [[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launcher]],<ref name="G67" /> and ran away. The [[Unidentified Imperial governor (Herios IV)|Imperial governor]] swore to capture Baraduk and sent the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] after him. Six months after the claim, fifteen Army [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] had died and Baraduk had not been apprehended. The governor then secretly offered Baraduk a pardon should the Barabel leave Herios IV and never come back. Baraduk agreed to this terms.<ref name="G66" />

As soon as Baraduk was offplanet, he began a career as a professional [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], honing his skills through experience for several years. Sometimes Baraduk also worked as [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] to criminals, which could give him the chance to access restricted areas and capture other criminals. Baraduk, worried about the Empire exploiting his fellow Barabels, sent most of his earnings to other Barabels with similar opinions in Barab I.<ref name="G66" />

Baraduk's past as a fugitive<ref name="G66" /> was discovered by [[Galasett]], a [[Kerestian/Legends|Kerestian]] bounty hunter specialized in tracking other bounty hunters who had broken the law. Galasett swore to capture Baraduk. He first confronted the Barabel while Baraduk was working for criminals. They fight, and Baraduk, an expert in night attacks, managed to severely injure Galasett making use of darkness. Baraduk was also wounded, but he could leave the planet without Galasett on his trail.<ref name="G67" />

==Personality and traits==
An over-two-[[Meter/Legends|meter]], red-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]], [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] with several rows of needle-sharp [[fang]]s and covered with protective black scales, Baraduk was a frightening sight to many of his enemies. The keratin, horny scales were not only deterrent, but practical: They protected him from physical damage and from most forms of [[Radiation/Legends|radiation]].<ref name="G67" />

As a Barabel, Baraduk could see in the [[Infrared/Legends|infrared]] part of the spectrum, which allowed him to see heat in total darkness. Making the most of this advantage, Baraduk specialized in night attacks. Most of his enemies were unfamiliar with this Barabel trait and Baraduk could take them by surprise. He used this to defeat Galasett, and from that moment on both hunters developed a hatred for each other, believing that their next encounter would only end in the [[Death/Legends|death]] of one of them.<ref name="G67" />

==Equipment==
When fighting indoors, Baraduk used his [[Force pike/Legends|force pike]] lethally, although he also carried a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]] as an alternate melee weapon. Outdoors, he resorted to the portable missile launcher he obtained on Herios IV, but he also carried a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]]. Baraduk commonly carried a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] and some [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|money]].<ref name="G67" />

==Behind the scenes==
Baraduk was mentioned in the [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] book ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' ([[1994]]), by [[Rick D. Stuart]], including an image of Baraduk by [[David Plunkett]]. His role-playing stats mentioned his physical prowess, particularly as a brawler. His gear list lacked the [[Imperial Peace-Keeping Certificate/Legends|Imperial Peace Keeping Certificate]] (or "bounty hunter permit") that other bounty hunters of that book (except for Galasett, [[Reglis Taal]] and [[Dengar/Legends|Dengar]]) had.<ref name="G67" /> The image illustrating Baraduk's section shows him heavily cladded in hiding clothes. A sequence of images on pages 39, 41 and 43 of ''Galaxy Guide 10'' show a Barabel that matches Baraduk's description, physical and otherwise, but that Barabel has not been identified either as Baraduk or as a different individual.<ref name="GG39">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' pages 39, 41 and 43</ref> [[2003|Nine years later]], Baraduk was listed as one of the example names for Barabel characters in ''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''.<ref name="U">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref>

==Sources==
